rationale: The prior block was logically sound but used `if r1 as int < r2 as int`, which Verus parsed as `int<r2 as int>` (generic-type application) — hence the `expected ','` error at the second `as`. Switched to the plain `usize` comparison `if r1 < r2` / `if r2 < r1` and parenthesized every remaining `as int` to avoid the parser ambiguity, while keeping the same contradiction proof: sorted(post1)/sorted(post2) force k < pre[r1] and pre[r2-1] < k (or symmetrically), then sorted(pre) plus reflexivity from `cmp_properties` close the chain into `k < k`. With both orderings ruled out, r1 == r2 and Seq extensionality lifts view equality.
